name: Midgard
description: Midgard, meaning "Middle Enclosure" or "Middle Earth," is the realm of humanity in Norse cosmology. It is one of the Nine Worlds, situated between Asgard, the home of the gods, and Jotunheim, the land of the giants. Midgard was created when Odin, Vili, and Ve slew the giant Ymir and fashioned the world from his body. His flesh became the earth, his blood the seas, his bones the mountains, and his skull the sky. Midgard is encircled by the vast ocean, in which the immense serpent Jormungandr, one of Loki's children, resides, biting its own tail. A rainbow bridge, Bifrost, connects Midgard to Asgard, providing a pathway for the gods to traverse between the realms. Midgard is destined to be destroyed during Ragnarok, the end of the world, when Jormungandr rises from the ocean, poisoning the land and sea, and the world is consumed by fire. Despite its eventual destruction, Midgard represents the realm of mortal existence, human civilization, and the struggles and triumphs of mankind in the face of fate and the powers of the gods and giants. The inhabitants of Midgard are constantly threatened by forces of chaos and destruction, requiring them to be resilient and resourceful.
subtitle: The Realm of Humanity in Norse Myth

keyMyths.title: Creation of Midgard
keyMyths.description: Odin and his brothers fashioned Midgard from the body of the slain giant Ymir. His flesh became the earth, his blood the sea, and his bones the mountains.

keyMyths.title: Jormungandr's Encirclement
keyMyths.description: The serpent Jormungandr encircles Midgard, biting its own tail, symbolizing the world's boundaries. Its release during Ragnarok signals the end of the world.

keyMyths.title: Bifrost Bridge
keyMyths.description: The rainbow bridge Bifrost connects Asgard and Midgard, allowing the gods to travel between the realms. Heimdall guards the bridge from giants and other threats.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, describing the creation and structure of Midgard.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, describing the creation and structure of Midgard.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse beliefs and myths.
